title: carex dianae var. aequabilis
intro: Carex dianae var. aequabilis, also known as mountain sedge, is a variety of sedge plant that belongs to the family Cyperaceae. It is a perennial grass-like plant native to high-altitude mountainous regions. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can be used to treat wounds and skin conditions. In herbal preparations, the plant can be made into teas, tinctures, or infusions to reap its health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting skin health, and supporting respiratory well-being.
